slides.heading: The Problem: Tool Pollution
slides.body: Every tool you expose competes for context in the agent's prompt. Benchmarks show accuracy drops from 87% to 77% as tools grow from 25 to 150. More tools = dumber agents.

slides.heading: The Old Model: 1 Endpoint = 1 Tool
slides.body: get_user, list_orders, get_shipment -- three tools, three round-trips. The agent figures out chaining. This is REST thinking applied to MCP. It does not work.

slides.heading: The Better Model: 1 Capability = 1 Tool
slides.body: track_latest_order(email) -- one call. User lookup, order fetch, status check all happen behind the facade. Think macros, not endpoints.

slides.heading: The Evidence
slides.body: GitHub's 40-tool server needed an escape hatch (60-90% context reduction when trimmed). Block collapsed 30+ tools into 2. Docker recommends macros after building 100+ servers.

slides.heading: The Rule: 2-4 Tools Per Server
slides.body: If you need more surface, split by domain. crypto-intelligence: research, compare. crypto-trading: timing, risk. Two servers, two trust boundaries, four focused capabilities.

slides.heading: How Many Tools Does Your MCP Server Expose?
slides.body: The answer matters more than which model you picked.
